title: oxalis sepium subsp. sepium
intro: Oxalis sepium subsp. sepium, also known as wood sorrel, is a plant species that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Oxalis sepium subsp. sepium include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve symptoms of arthritis, and support urinary tract health. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots can also be used fresh in salads and as a garnish.
